title: Bridge Essay: What is mood? A computational perspective.
concept: Predictive processing — free energy principle, active inference, Bayesian brain.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Dependent Arising** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Dependent arising — predictive processing, free energy principle, active inference as causal cognition.
**Phase context:** Predictive processing — free energy principle, active inference, Bayesian brain.

**What is mood? A computational perspective.** — Psychol Med (2018). DOI: 10.1017/S0033291718000430. PMID: 29478431. The neurobiological understanding of mood, and by extension mood disorders, remains elusive despite decades of research implicating several neuromodulator systems. This review considers a new approach based on existing theories of functional brain organisation. The free energy principle (a.k.a. active inference), and its instantiation in the Bayesian brain, offers a complete and simple formulation of mood.
